title: Oerstedella lactea

The plant has been found to exhibit several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce the risk of infections, and promote wound healing. taxonomy: Oerstedella lactea is classified as a species of flowering orchid in the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it is a member of the genus Oerstedella, which is a group of epiphytic orchids native to Central and South America. The classification of Oerstedella lactea is further refined to the tribe Cranichideae, a subfamily of orchids characterized by their unique flower structures. The species is also assigned to the subfamily Spiranthoideae, which contains many epiphytic orchids with similar growth habits. Overall, the classification of Oerstedella lactea reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other orchids.
